headline: JKR project delays cost government RM3.8 billion in 2025
context: Public Works Department project delays resulted in RM3.8 billion in cost overruns and extension of time claims across 142 federal infrastructure projects in 2025.

cards.big: 142 JKR projects delayed — RM3.8 billion in cost overruns charged to taxpayers
cards.sub: Chronic project delays have become the most expensive norm in Malaysian governance

cards.big: PWD Standard Form of Contract allows unlimited extensions of time
cards.sub: Contractual framework incentivises delay — contractors face no meaningful penalties

cards.big: Average cost overrun was 27% across delayed projects in 2025
cards.sub: For every RM100 budgeted, taxpayers pay RM127 due to systematic delay culture

cards.big: JKR's project management information system flagged 89 at-risk projects 6 months early
cards.sub: Early warning system exists but intervention mechanism does not — information without action

cards.big: RM3.8 billion in overruns isn't bad luck — it's predictable failure in a system that doesn't penalise delay
Should contractors face automatic liquidated damages for project delays without valid force majeure?
